title: L3: Conditional Functions
description: IF, COUNTIF, SUMIF and percentages for BTEC DIT Component 2 Task 2b.

steps.title: The IF Function
steps.content:
The IF function: making a decision

Sometimes a cell should show different text depending on a value. For example: “if income is above £350, show Top Seller; otherwise show Standard.” That is exactly what IF does.

Syntax (three parts, separated by commas):

=IF(condition, value_if_true, value_if_false)

Example: =IF(C2>350, "Top Seller", "Standard")

IncomeCondition C2>350Result
404.55TRUETop Seller
334.33FALSEStandard

Common condition operators:

SymbolMeaning
>Greater than
<Less than
>=Greater than or equal to
=Equal to (inside IF, = means equals)

Your turn

In D2, write a decision formula: if the Income is above 350, show Top Seller; otherwise show Standard. Replicate it down to D3:D6, then click Check.

The condition should test whether the Income in that row is above 350.';return;}if(ok<5){fb.className='ex-feedback err';fb.textContent='Some rows are wrong. Check the condition references the correct column (C) in each row.';return;}fb.className='ex-feedback ok';fb.textContent='Correct! IF automatically applies the right label to each row based on the income.';__markStepComplete();};});});});})();

steps.title: COUNTIF
steps.content:
COUNTIF: count cells that match a condition

=COUNT counts all numbers. =COUNTIF counts only cells that match a specific condition. For example, how many products are sold in the North region.

Syntax:

=COUNTIF(range, criteria)

=COUNTIF(B2:B6, "North") counts cells in B2:B6 that say “North”

FormulaWhat it counts
=COUNTIF(B2:B6, "North")Cells in B2:B6 containing the text North
=COUNTIF(C2:C6, ">350")Cells in C2:C6 with a value above 350

Your turn

Add formulas in the summary rows:

  • C7: count how many Region cells say North.
  • C8: count how many Region cells say South.

North=3 (rows 2,4,6) and South=2 (rows 3,5). Check the criteria match exactly.';return;}fb.className='ex-feedback ok';fb.textContent='Correct! COUNTIF(B2:B6,"North")=3 and COUNTIF(B2:B6,"South")=2. COUNTIF counts by category.';__markStepComplete();};});});});})();

steps.title: SUMIF
steps.content:
SUMIF: add values matching a condition

=SUM adds everything. =SUMIF adds only the values where a corresponding cell matches your condition. For example: the total income from North products only.

Syntax (three parts):

=SUMIF(range_to_check, criteria, range_to_sum)

=SUMIF(B2:B6, "North", C2:C6)

“Where column B says North, add the matching values from column C.”

Your turn

  • C7: add the Income values for rows where Region is North.
  • C8: add the Income values for rows where Region is South.

North total=888.73 (rows 2,4,6), South total=843.40 (rows 3,5). Check your ranges and criteria.';return;}fb.className='ex-feedback ok';fb.textContent='Correct! North total=£888.73, South total=£843.40. SUMIF is one of the most useful BTEC analysis functions.';__markStepComplete();};});});});})();

steps.title: Percentages
steps.content:
Percentages: share of the total

In BTEC you often need to show each product’s income as a percentage of the total:

=B2/B$7

“This product’s income ÷ total income, then format as a percentage”

The $ matters. When you copy this formula down, B2 adjusts to B3, B4, etc. That is expected: each row should reference its own income. But B7 (the total) must never move. Adding $ before the row number locks it: B$7. This is called an absolute reference.

CellFormulaResult
C2=B2/B$723.35%
C3 (copied)=B3/B$727.69%

Your turn

The total is in B7. In C2, calculate Phone Cases as a percentage of the total, then replicate it down to C3:C6. Remember to keep the total row fixed when you copy down. Click Check when all five are done.
steps.js: (function(){function lcss(u){if(!document.querySelector('link[href="'+u+'"]')){var l=document.createElement('link');l.rel='stylesheet';l.href=u;document.head.appendChild(l);}}function ljs(u,cb){var ex=document.querySelector('script[data-jhncc-src="'+u+'"]');if(ex){if(ex.getAttribute('data-loaded')==='1'){cb();}else{ex.addEventListener('load',function(){cb();});}return;}var s=document.createElement('script');s.setAttribute('data-jhncc-src',u);s.src=u;s.onload=function(){s.setAttribute('data-loaded','1');cb();};s.onerror=function(){alert('The spreadsheet tool could not load. Please refresh the page.');};document.head.appendChild(s);}lcss('assets/css/jsuites.css');lcss('assets/css/jspreadsheet.css');ljs('assets/js/jsuites.js',function(){ljs('assets/js/jspreadsheet.js',function(){ljs('assets/js/jspreadsheet-formula-bar.js?v=22',function(){var data=[["Item","Income (£)","% of Total"],["Phone Cases",404.55,""],["Earphones",479.68,""],["Screen Protectors",334.33,""],["Chargers",363.72,""],["Phone Stands",149.85,""],["TOTAL","=SUM(B2:B6)",""]];var sheet=jspreadsheet(document.getElementById('l3-pct-sheet'),{data:data,columns:[{"width":155},{"width":110},{"width":120}],minDimensions:[3,7],tableOverflow:true,tableWidth:'100%',toolbar:false,about:false});JHNCCAddFormulaBar(document.getElementById('l3-pct-sheet'),sheet);JHNCCAddFormatToolbar(document.getElementById('l3-pct-sheet'),sheet);JHNCCAddSheetTabs(document.getElementById('l3-pct-sheet'),sheet);document.getElementById('l3-pct-check').onclick=function(){var fb=document.getElementById('l3-pct-fb');var incomes=[404.55,479.68,334.33,363.72,149.85];var total=1732.13;var ok=0,noFormula=false;for(var row=1;row<=5;row++){var raw=String(sheet.getValueFromCoords(2,row,false)||'');var val=parseFloat(sheet.getValueFromCoords(2,row,true));if(raw.charAt(0)!=='='){noFormula=true;break;}var exp=incomes[row-1]/total;var fmt=(sheet._jhnccNumberFormats&&sheet._jhnccNumberFormats['C'+(row+1)]&&sheet._jhnccNumberFormats['C'+(row+1)].type)||'general';if(Math.abs(val-exp)<0.001&&fmt==='percent')ok++;}if(noFormula){fb.className='ex-feedback hint';fb.textContent='Add a percentage-of-total formula in C2 and replicate it down to C3:C6. The total should stay fixed on row 7 when copied, then format C2:C6 as Percentage.';return;}if(ok<5){fb.className='ex-feedback err';fb.textContent='Some percentages are wrong. Use part divided by total, keep B$7 fixed, and format C2:C6 as Percentage.';return;}fb.className='ex-feedback ok';fb.textContent='Correct! The formulas calculate proportions and the cells are formatted as percentages.';__markStepComplete();};});});});})();

steps.title: Full Practice
steps.content:
Full practice: IF, COUNTIF and SUMIF together

Complete all four tasks below using the dataset provided. Choose the correct conditional function and range for each task.

TaskCellWhat to calculate
1D2:D6Label each product as Top Seller if its Income is above 350, otherwise Standard.
2C8Count the products in the North region.
3C9Add the Income values for the South region.
4C10Count how many Income values are above 350.

data=[["Item","Region","Income (£)","Status"],["Phone Cases","North",404.55,""],["Earphones","South",479.68,""],["Screen Protectors","North",334.33,""],["Chargers","South",363.72,""],["Phone Stands","North",149.85,""],["TOTAL","","=SUM(C2:C6)",""],["North count","","",""],["South total","","",""],["Income > 350 count","","",""]];var sheet=jspreadsheet(document.getElementById('l3-all-sheet'),{data:data,columns:[{"width":175},{"width":80},{"width":110},{"width":130}],minDimensions:[4,10],tableOverflow:true,tableWidth:'100%',toolbar:false,about:false});JHNCCAddFormulaBar(document.getElementById('l3-all-sheet'),sheet);JHNCCAddFormatToolbar(document.getElementById('l3-all-sheet'),sheet);JHNCCAddSheetTabs(document.getElementById('l3-all-sheet'),sheet);document.getElementById('l3-all-check').onclick=function(){var fb=document.getElementById('l3-all-fb');var errors=[];var ifOk=0;for(var row=1;row<=5;row++){var raw=String(sheet.getValueFromCoords(3,row,false)||'');var val=String(sheet.getValueFromCoords(3,row,true)||'').toLowerCase().trim();var inc=parseFloat(sheet.getValueFromCoords(2,row,true));if(raw.charAt(0)==='='&&raw.toUpperCase().indexOf('IF(')!==-1&&val===(inc>350?'top seller':'standard'))ifOk++;}if(ifOk<5)errors.push('Task 1: IF in D2:D6 is incomplete or wrong ('+ifOk+'/5 rows correct)');var r8=String(sheet.getValueFromCoords(2,7,false)||'');var v8=parseFloat(sheet.getValueFromCoords(2,7,true));if(r8.toUpperCase().indexOf('COUNTIF')===-1)errors.push('Task 2: use COUNTIF in C8');else if(Math.abs(v8-3)>0.5)errors.push('Task 2: North count should be 3, got '+v8);var r9=String(sheet.getValueFromCoords(2,8,false)||'');var v9=parseFloat(sheet.getValueFromCoords(2,8,true));if(r9.toUpperCase().indexOf('SUMIF')===-1)errors.push('Task 3: use SUMIF in C9');else if(Math.abs(v9-843.40)>0.5)errors.push('Task 3: South total should be 843.40, got '+v9);var r10=String(sheet.getValueFromCoords(2,9,false)||'');var v10=parseFloat(sheet.getValueFromCoords(2,9,true));if(r10.toUpperCase().indexOf('COUNTIF')===-1)errors.push('Task 4: use COUNTIF in C10');else if(Math.abs(v10-3)>0.5)errors.push('Task 4: count of income>350 should be 3, got '+v10);if(errors.length){fb.className='ex-feedback err';fb.textContent='Issues: '+errors.join(' | ');return;}fb.className='ex-feedback ok';fb.textContent='All four tasks correct! IF, COUNTIF and SUMIF are the core conditional functions in BTEC Task 2b.';__markStepComplete();};});});});})();

steps.title: Check What You Have Learned
steps.content:
Check what you have learned

Feedback appears after each question.
steps.js: createMCQSet('l3-exam',[{q:'=IF(B2>100, "Pass", "Fail"). B2=80. What is returned?',options:['Fail','Pass','80','TRUE'],answer:0,right:'Correct. 80 is not greater than 100, so the third part is returned.'},{q:'What does =COUNTIF(B2:B10, "North") do?',options:['Counts cells in B2:B10 that contain North','Adds up values where B says North','Checks if North exists in B2','Returns the position of North'],answer:0,right:'Correct. COUNTIF counts cells that match the criteria.'},{q:'=SUMIF(B2:B6, "South", C2:C6). What does this add?',options:['C values where the matching B cell says South','All values in C2:C6','B values that say South','All values in both columns'],answer:0,right:'Correct. SUMIF adds the C values where the matching B cell meets the criteria.'},{q:'You copy =B2/B$7 from C2 to C3. What does C3 contain?',options:['=B3/B$7','=B3/B8','=B2/B$7','=B3/B7'],answer:0,right:'Correct. B3 adjusts because it is relative, but B$7 stays locked because the row is absolute.'},{q:'Which formula counts cells in D2:D10 with a value greater than 500?',options:['=COUNTIF(D2:D10,">500")','=COUNT(D2:D10,">500")','=COUNTIF(D2:D10,500)','=IF(D2:D10>500)'],answer:0,right:'Correct. The criteria goes in quotes: ">500".'},{q:'What is the purpose of $ in B$7?',options:['Locks row 7 so it does not change when the formula is copied down','Formats the cell as currency','Indicates a negative value','Separates arguments'],answer:0,right:'Correct. The $ locks that part of the reference so it does not change when copied.'}]);

steps.title: Endless Practice
steps.content:
Endless practice

Answer 5 in a row to finish. A wrong answer resets the streak.
steps.js:
(function(){var bank=[{q:'=IF(A2>50,"Yes","No"). A2=30. What is returned?',o:['No','Yes','30','TRUE'],a:0},{q:'=IF(A2>50,"Yes","No"). A2=70. What is returned?',o:['Yes','No','70','FALSE'],a:0},{q:'Which function counts cells matching a condition?',o:['COUNTIF','COUNT','SUMIF','IF'],a:0},{q:'Which function sums only cells where a condition is met?',o:['SUMIF','SUM','COUNTIF','IF'],a:0},{q:'=SUMIF(B2:B6,"East",C2:C6). What does it add up?',o:['C values where B says East','B values where B says East','All of C2:C6','All of B2:B6'],a:0},{q:'How do you lock row 7 in a cell reference?',o:['B$7','$B7','B7$','$$7'],a:0},{q:'=COUNTIF(D2:D20,">100"). What does this count?',o:['Cells in D2:D20 with a value above 100','Cells equal to 100','All numeric cells','Cells below 100'],a:0},{q:'In =IF(condition, A, B), when is B returned?',o:['When the condition is FALSE','When the condition is TRUE','Always','Never'],a:0},{q:'You copy =C2/C$7 from D2 to D3. What does D3 contain?',o:['=C3/C$7','=C2/C$7','=C3/C8','=C3/C7'],a:0}];var solved=0,c=document.getElementById('l3-endless');function pick(){return bank[Math.floor(Math.random()*bank.length)];}function render(){var q=pick();var opts=q.o.map(function(x,i){return{x:x,i:i};}).sort(function(){return Math.random()-0.5;});c.innerHTML='

Correct in a row: '+solved+' / 5

'+q.q+'

'+opts.map(function(o){return '';}).join('')+'
';c.querySelectorAll('button').forEach(function(btn){btn.onclick=function(){var fb=c.querySelector('.ex-feedback');if(parseInt(btn.dataset.i)===q.a){solved++;fb.className='ex-feedback ok';fb.textContent=solved>=5?'Practice complete! Well done.':'Correct! Next question.';if(solved>=5)__markStepComplete();else setTimeout(render,700);}else{solved=0;fb.className='ex-feedback err';fb.textContent='Not quite. Streak reset. Try again.';setTimeout(render,1100);}};});} render();})();

quizQuestions.q: In =IF(C2>350,"Top Seller","Standard"), what is returned when C2 is 334.33?
In D2 and D3, label each product. If its Income is above 350, show Top Seller. Otherwise show Standard.
Complete D4:D6 with the same decision: Income above 350 is Top Seller; otherwise Standard.

options: Cells in B2:B6 that contain North

quizQuestions.q: In C7, count how many products are in the North region.
In C8, add the Income values for products in the South region.
In C9, count how many Income values are greater than 350.
The total is in B7. In C2, calculate Phone Cases as a percentage of the total by dividing income by total. Format C2 as Percentage.
